segments.text: "Now Elijah the Tishbite, who was among the settlers of Gilead,[^123] said to Ahab,
As surely as the LORD lives—the God of Israel before whom I st and—there will be neither dew nor rain in these years except at my word!
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: "Then a revelation from the LORD came to Elijah:
Leave here, turn eastward, and hide yourself by the Brook of Cherith, east of the Jordan.[^124]
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And you are to drink from the brook, and I have commanded the ravens to feed you there.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: "So Elijah did what the LORD had told him, and he went and lived by the Brook of Cherith, east of the Jordan.
"The ravens would bring him bread and meat in the morning and evening, and he would drink from the brook.
"Some time later, however, the brook dried up because there had been no rain in the land.
"Then the word of the LORD came to Elijah:
Get up and go to Zarephath of Sidon, and stay there. Behold, I have commanded a widow there to provide for you.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: "So Elijah got up and went to Zarephath. When he arrived at the city gate, there was a widow gathering sticks. Elijah called to her and said,
Please bring me a little water in a cup, so that I may drink.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: "And as she was going to get it, he called to her and said,
Please bring me a piece of bread.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: "But she replied,

As surely as the LORD your God lives, I have no bread—only a handful of flour in a jar and a little oil in a jug. Look, I am gathering a couple of sticks to take home and prepare a meal for myself and my son, so that we may eat it and die.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Do not be afraid,
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Elijah said to her.
Go and do as you have said. But first make me a small cake of bread from what you have, and bring it out to me. Afterward, make some for yourself and your son,
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: for this is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says: ‘The jar of flour will not be exhausted and the jug of oil will not run dry until the day the LORD sends rain upon the face of the earth.’
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: "So she went and did according to the word of Elijah, and there was food every day for Elijah and the woman and her household.
"The jar of flour was not exhausted and the jug of oil did not run dry, according to the word that the LORD had spoken through Elijah.
"Later, the son of the woman who owned the house became ill, and his sickness grew worse and worse, until no breath remained in him.
O man of God,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: said the woman to Elijah,
what have you done to me? Have you come to remind me of my iniquity and cause the death of my son?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: "But Elijah said to her,
Give me your son.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So he took him from her arms, carried him to the upper room where he was staying, and laid him on his own bed.
"Then he cried out to the LORD,
O LORD my God, have You also brought tragedy on this widow who has opened her home to me, by causing her son to die?
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: "Then he stretched himself out over the child three times and cried out to the LORD,
O LORD my God, please let this boy’s life return to him!
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: "And the LORD listened to the voice of Elijah, and the child’s life returned to him, and he lived.
"Then Elijah took the child, brought him down from the upper room into the house, and gave him to his mother.
Look, your son is alive,
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Elijah declared.
"Then the woman said to Elijah,
Now I know that you are a man of God and that the word of the LORD from your mouth is truth.
segments.speaker: Elijah
